marketState: bearish near-term with potential weather-driven reversal developing in 1-2 weeks
summary: Front NG=F at $3.198 trades $0.24 above the current fair-value midpoint of $2.958 — slightly rich but comfortably within the $2.458–$3.458 band, and that premium compresses on its own as fair value rises to $3.064 by day 21 (front then reads 'FAIR'). Weather creates a stark two-window split: next 1-7 days demand is 20.7% below the 5-year average (a genuine near-term bearish headwind), while days 8-16 are running 51.3% above the 5-year average and have already breached the 5-year maximum — an extraordinary heat surge that can ignite short covering quickly. Storage at 2,759 Bcf is +153 Bcf above the 5-year average, and last week's injection of +73 Bcf came in 25 Bcf above the 48 Bcf forecast — a fresh bearish data point — yet managed-money net positioning sits at just the 3rd percentile of its 5-year range (-122,617 contracts), making the market a compressed spring for a short squeeze. Seasonality is unreliable (44 pp 10–90% spread) but shows 69% probability of higher prices in 3 weeks, while the futures curve's deep winter contango ($4.05–4.45 for Dec–Jan) signals the market already anticipates strong seasonal demand ahead.

longTerm.rationale: Managed-money net short at the 3rd percentile of the 5-year range (-122,617 contracts, mmRatio 0.61) is the dominant structural signal: the crowd is as short as it has been in years, requiring only a catalyst to trigger covering. The 8-16D weather demand at +51.3% vs 5-year and above the 5-year maximum provides exactly that catalyst, and seasonality gives 69% probability of higher prices in 3 weeks. The fair-value band upper edge at $3.46–$3.56 over the next three weeks and winter futures at $4.05–4.45 (Dec–Jan 2027) define realistic upside targets without requiring a fundamental repricing.
longTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $2.90 (clear break below fair-value midpoint), storage builds consistently exceeding 80 Bcf/week pushing the 5y surplus beyond 200 Bcf, or 8-16D weather forecast revising sharply cooler before the heat becomes established

shortTerm.rationale: Next 1-7 days weather demand sits 20.7% below the 5-year average in only the 22nd percentile of the range — a genuine fundamental headwind that limits cooling-driven buying pressure through most of next week. The storage report (73 Bcf build vs 48 Bcf forecast) is fresh bearish news and price is already $0.24 above the fair-value midpoint. Taking the short side is unattractive given COT at the 3rd percentile and extreme 8-16D heat incoming; taking the long side now risks absorbing near-term weather weakness before the catalyst arrives.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter LONG on either: (a) a pullback to $3.05–$3.10 with intraday stabilization — buying near fair-value midpoint ahead of the incoming heat wave with improved risk-reward; or (b) a daily close above $3.28 on improving weather revisions or confirmed short-squeeze momentum visible in 1h/4h technicals
